title: Enticing Ethiopian Tomato Custard
description: A sour, juicy, kid-friendly Ethiopian dessert featuring vanilla coconut custard and steamed mushimono, dairy-free and easy to make.
instructions: Heat a medium saucepan over medium heat and sauté the diced tomatoes until they soften, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the vanilla extract, lemon juice, ginger, paprika, and salt, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
In a separate bowl, whisk the coconut milk, maple syrup, cornstarch, and water until smooth.
Pour the coconut mixture into the saucepan, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer, whisking frequently until the custard thickens, about 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and let the custard cool slightly while preparing the mushimono.
Place the mushimono into a steamer basket and steam over boiling water for 8 minutes until tender.
Transfer the steamed mushimono to a serving dish and pour the tomato custard over it.
Garnish with chopped basil leaves for a fresh finish.
Serve warm for a kid-friendly dessert or snack.
